notes: The doctrinally load-bearing terms in 2.38 are sukha-duḥkhe (felt experience), lābhālābhau (gain/loss), jayājayau (victory/defeat), same kṛtvā (treating-as the same), yuddhāya yujyasva (engage in battle), and naivaṁ pāpam avāpsyasi (thus you will not incur sin / bondage). The engineering layer preserves all six WITH careful structural fidelity. The three pairs are mapped to three distinct registers (felt experience as Datadog moves; launch hits/misses target; competitor outcome) — they remain three distinct pairs, NOT collapsed into one. The 'same kṛtvā' is rendered as 'identical dispositional posture toward all three categories,' which preserves Shankara's gloss ('without likes and dislikes') without collapsing the pairs. The 'yujyasva' is rendered as 'engage' — preserving the imperative. The 'pāpam' is rendered (following Ramanuja) as 'bondage,' specifically the engineering form of saṁsāric bondage (burnout, blame-displacement, defensive code) — not narrow moral fault. Shankara's, Ramanuja's, and Sridhara's readings are all named in the source pack and not conflated. CRITICALLY: the foreshadowing of 2.47 and 2.48 is honored without copying — the engineering layer says 'arrive identical' (the dispositional content of yoga-sthaḥ) without using the technical term, and explicitly notes 2.38 is the SEED of doctrines that 2.47 and 2.48 elaborate. PASS.
